materials.name: ARGAN OIL
materials.notes: No acute 2026 event, but real ongoing STRUCTURAL supply risk: chronic multi-year drought is reducing argan fruit yield per tree and oil extraction rates, compounded by overharvesting of wild argan forests. Fragmented supply chain (multiple intermediaries between harvester cooperatives and exporters) limits cooperatives ability to hold inventory and negotiate, keeping farmgate prices volatile/low even as retail argan oil prices stay high. A new government storage-center program is expected in 2026 aiming to ease this, outcome unconfirmed.

materials.name: BASIL OIL (METHYLCHAVICOL TYPE) METHYL EUGENOL FREE
Black Pepper Oil
materials.notes: No acute disruption identified as of June 2026. Note a real structural interlink: Indonesian farmers (esp. Sulawesi) shift distillation infrastructure between Patchouli and Clove oil depending on seasonal clove-bud harvest timing, so the two materials are not fully independent in supply - a clove harvest year can temporarily reduce patchouli oil output as farmers redirect stills, and vice versa.

materials.name: CARDAMOM ABSOLUTE
materials.notes: No major acute disruption as of mid-June 2026 - black pepper and cardamom prices reported stable-to-easing in domestic/export markets. Ongoing structural issue: Indian exporters report a container shortage and rising freight costs affecting spice exports broadly (logistics cost pressure, not a supply/crop event).

materials.name: Chamomile Oil Italian
materials.notes: No acute supply/crop disruption identified. Real current logistics/compliance risk: Egypt requires a mandatory digital customs system (Nafeza/ACI) for all imports and exports as of 2026 - shipments from suppliers unfamiliar with the system can be held at port for weeks with fines, risking spoilage of perishable aromatics. This is a supplier-competence/logistics risk rather than a price-driving event.

materials.name: Clove Bud Oil
materials.notes: Madagascar (Sava region, ~80% of world vanilla supply) hit by Cyclone Gezani, Feb 2026 - landfall as Cat 3 equivalent, ~$2B damage, 59 dead in-country. Came 10 days after Cyclone Fytia. Vanilla orchids take ~4 years to reach harvest, so repeated storm damage compounds across seasons. Clove (same Sava region) likely faces logistics/labor disruption even though the plant itself is less storm-fragile than vanilla vines.

materials.name: Galbanum Oil
materials.notes: No current sourcing-specific disruption identified via search. Iran is a long-standing source of Galbanum resin; general awareness of US/Western sanctions on Iran remains a standing background risk for any Iran-origin trade, but no Galbanum-specific 2026 disruption was found in this pass - worth a follow-up search with different terms if this material matters significantly to your sourcing.

materials.name: Geranium Oil
materials.notes: No acute disruption identified as of June 2026. Geranium oil production concentrated in Yunnan Province (China is the world's largest producer) - normal seasonal trade, no current event flagged.

materials.name: Ginger Oil
materials.notes: No major acute disruption as of mid-June 2026 - black pepper and cardamom prices reported stable-to-easing in domestic/export markets. materials.name: Guaiacwood Oil (CITES)
materials.notes: No acute disruption identified. Structural/regulatory note: Guaiacwood (the primary Paraguay-sourced material here) is CITES-restricted due to historical overharvesting of the Bulnesia sarmienti tree - this caps/regulates available supply by design, distinct from a market shock, but worth knowing as a standing constraint on volume.

materials.name: Myrrh Oil
materials.notes: Frankincense/Myrrh harvesting is concentrated in Somaliland and Puntland (northern Somalia), which function largely independent of the conflict-affected south and have an active, ongoing export trade as of June 2026 - no acute current disruption identified. Structurally fragile long-term (harvester communities in conflict-adjacent areas, climate pressure reducing moisture for the Boswellia/Commiphora trees) but not in active crisis right now.
